target.name: Majestic Labs
target.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Majestic Labs is a Series A chip design company in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, having raised $100M+. The valuation analysis draws on two applicable triangulation methods against a peer set comprising 4 private same-stage comparables (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) and 3 public semiconductor reference points (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Stage-progression methodology, anchoring to the $100M total capital raised as a proxy for current enterprise value, applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for Series A progression, implying a $200M valuation. The peer-median approach across the 4 private comparables yields $1.5B, with the peer set ranging from $500M (Rain AI) to $5.0B (Etched). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied given the absence of revenue disclosure for private companies in this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $170M to $1.7B, with a median of $850M across the two applicable methods. The wide spread reflects meaningful dispersion in peer valuations and the sensitivity of stage-based multiples to assumed growth rates. Key uncertainties include whether Majestic Labs' technology differentiation and market timing warrant positioning toward the upper quartile of peer valuations, and the absence of revenue or unit economics limits confidence in fundamental anchoring. Stage-progression alone ($200M) sits below the peer median, suggesting either relative early-stage positioning or valuation uplift potential if productization and customer traction materialize.
